title: A community-codesigned LLM-powered chatbot for primary care: a randomized controlled trial
core_conclusion: Pairing an LLM-powered primary-care chatbot (P&P Care, GPT-4o mini) with short community-codesigned e-learning on how to interact with AI significantly raised patients' objective health awareness (2.95 vs 2.34; P<0.001) in a pragmatic RCT of 2,113 urban and rural participants across 11 Chinese provinces. A dual-track role-play codesign framework closed AI-literacy and connectivity gaps and let the chatbot outperform usual care and telemedicine, offering a scalable route to deploy LLMs in resource-limited settings.
why_it_matters: Moves patient-facing LLMs beyond hospitals into rural/resource-limited communities; pinpoints AI health literacy (not model size) as the bottleneck; quantifies disproportionate gains for older and multimorbid patients and a residual rural divide; narrows the self-reported vs objective health-awareness gap; and ships a transferable codesign + connectivity-hardening recipe.
why_nature: Prospectively registered, CONSORT-AI, double-blinded RCT giving gold-standard evidence; strong equity and translational reach into LMIC primary care aligned with WHO people-centred care; methodologically novel dual-track role-play codesign; and candid about limitations (rural under-performance, dialect gaps, proxy comparators).
added_value_to_my_research: Chatbot as virtual supply term in 2SFCA/R2SFCA accessibility for primary-care deserts
